Inbox (To Process)

Raw Thoughts from 2/23/21 posted to slack

#rawthoughts from tonight - POSTED ON http://productfeedback.jacobcole.net/ – Feel free to comment there?

- #tweak typing speed even slow on brand new macbook (possibly a #bigdeal?)

- can’t see date on filtered view – obscured by jump to. probably also should have date dividers on filtered view if they’re on

- caps sensitivity on tags is still throwing me off (any objections to just moving fast and breaking things and making an executive decision on a fix here, like “capitalize the same tag closest to bottom of thoughtstream”?)

- selecting halfway into a note to end of note shouldn’t automatically merge me with the next note (#tweak the following action should leave one extra linebreak, I think: https://share.getcloudapp.com/RBu4YR9K)

- Date dividers got automatically turned off with the update. I think this might confuse a few of the people I on boarded FYI. It would be great if we can persist this in the future (does anyone object to having them be on by default?)

- Also this may have already been reported but it doesn’t seem that the auto sync via  checkpointing (without refresh) works fully on mobile. When I edit some thing on desktop and have  mobile open it doesn’t show up

- Command shift down arrow select all notes which is a bit scary (as does cmd shift up)

-  Dictation mysteriously not working sometimes on mobile  web

-  Back button works to go back from search results but forward button doesn’t work to go forward. Also, If you go two tags deep, and then hit the back button twice, it doesn’t take you home – you have to hit it an extra time to get back home #bug. Also, what would it take to maintain scroll position? easy? hard?

-----

More #polish I hope #quick

-  change the title to “Ideaflow” from “IdeaFlow”

-  maybe make the loading page allow user to start typing in the search bar and also have a blank editor that allows user to capture new thoughts, without having to wait?  (Maybe the blank editor should have placeholder text saying “Quick capture thoughts – notes are loading”?

-  #shortcut to:  clear search? Also another move to bottom / top of current cell (opt-down?)

-  #nitpick create new note shortcut (for now cmd+shift+k) should work even from Settings page. Can’t break that muscle memory /anywhere/! Also, can we have a link  to “pop out” the shortcuts cheat sheet somehow? Maybe into a new window? (edited)

------------

Other

#tweak

When you click on an <> relation it should flash the matched item and scroll it Roughly the top of the page, instead of leaving it in the middle. Actually, on my fairly large 4K monitor the same seems to be not working so well on any "jump to" operation. Also need to have a multi step back button behavior after such jump operations

Question -- If the app goes off-line does it warn me it’s not going to save my stuff somehow?

cmd+up arrow (maybe twice? Maybe first jumps you to top of note?) should jump you to the top even if you have really large note database. So should create new note shortcut. The only way for me to do so right now is to click All Notes

If you create a new blank page it should probably show something in the search bar and make it clear how you can go back? Maybe pages should be called sections. Or meetings / conversations?

Where would we slide in a "hide completed todos" checkbox on Todos page?

Sort <> relations by most recent and exact string match or i can’t find them (or maybe make 2 sections of results, exact followed by inexact?)

Experiment with trailing line breaks on notes instead of just divider? More like text file         ``

-------------

Pin feature ~spencer

Bulleted lists and subbullets  ~spencer #p1.5

Meeting Notes

#meeting Jacob <> Ben Williams 2020-12-15

#Legend

  Highlight  . Means transferred to Linear.app

  Highlight   Means transferred to Notion Product Design Discussion Logged Insights

P0

P1 – NEEDED FOR LAUNCH TO 1 ALPHA USER

on mobile web, we have duplicate hashtags (even with the same case)

we should normalize for case insensitive hashtags

Type a note with a hashtag filter on, sometimes the new note does not appear at the top (tough to replicate)

“When ‘eidetic memory deactivated’ error comes up say: ‘Don’t worry it’s already been reported.”

#tweak cmd click on tags to open link (or hover over and click "open link" like current urls) . I think just clicking is fine. https://workflowy.com and https://bear.app/ do that. (Maybe we could even make urls directly clickable? Not necessarily sure…)

#design Larger click target for clearing search criteria somehow – maybe back btn like mobile? @designer

When I try to type on my phone after the #gr, I can’t get the cursor to move there. I also can’t delete #gr. User error or bug in the app? https://share.getcloudapp.com/yAuZ8jJl ~jen tsau

 #small URLs get duplicated when you copy paste an entry with a URL

Pasting URLs out, also doubles the URL in Google Docs

#small? if it's ever not going to save if your work it should warn you
-in big warning message in real time when your note isn’t going to be saved. In addition to saying “JWT web token expired”. Maybe “WARNING: [Your login has expired OR You are offline ], your note will not be saved. If you want to capture it you must copy paste it to another application. (Will be fixed in future versions.)”
-On closing the page, a confirm dialog “Warning, if you close this page your changes will not be saved. Do you wish to proceed? Ok/cancel)
-? also, have a “Saving…” notification somewhere ~Wendell

#small? Browser Back button should return you to search results after you’ve hit jump. (#future Any ideas how to make a back button evident when you’re in a standalone chrome app? Maybe top left like notion. https://share.getcloudapp.com/WnulkGNn ) #design

#small? Scroll position on jump not working all the time ends you up before or after target which isn’t useful. Jumped item should be at the top of the page? Or maybe a little bit down is ok too.
        ?possibly highlight?)  jump not working all the time/targets the wrong one

#small? Reinstate cmd/ctrl-f for normal find in page? Maybe we can take cmd+shift+f for our search?

#mobile #small?  Way to see date dividers on filtered view on mobile (unable to recreate)
Can’t click on the i button on mobile to see date

Autocomplete should not match “@ [space]” or “~ [space ]” or “spaceship [space]’

After a @ sign, if you close the autocomplete window, you cannot double click on the text that would match with the autocomplete

if you close @ you can't double click on text autocomplete https://share.getcloudapp.com/geuop26R

#small? UX Polish On #mobile Web:

-Tapping in the margins doesn’t move cursor, like a text file does
-Seemingly impossible to tap the cursor so it begins at start of doc
-Select all selects everything whereas most of the time I wanted to just select a note. Moreover I can’t deselect

-share / download  button needs to allow me to share, or at least copy vs giving me a text file. [a]

?? #maybeDone Quick login and stay logged in on mobile

Hashtags now match literally on search e.g. #idea matches “idea”. Change this back to only matching “#idea”

User onboarding tutorial / walkthrough or at least some notes ~jm

https://docs.google.com/document/d/1yFaQt5S8eMMLkp1ZdhQe4rzcJzpN78xayynAKN3DNHs/edit#heading=h.wi7pm9prck2k

e.g. make it copy

-Old urls aren’t linkified

  P1.5 - on mobile web, there is no way to create a note at the bottom or after a current note (e.g. on filtered view)

Can’t scroll to see older notes

Cmd+a twice should not be limited to 500-odd notes

I’d like a fog over everything except the new note ~danish

 - ability to search on multiple tags by clicking on one after another (like ios). Needs some design  #design

Orig Inbox from Ben (Not all are P1, just notes I took from what jacob said, notes that are my own are ~Ben Williams)

 - Type a note with a hashtag filter on, sometimes the new note does not appear at the top

 - on mobile web, there is no way to create a note at the bottom or after a current note (e.g. on filtered view)

 - on mobile web, need the hashtag, mention, etc. bar at the bottom on top of the keyboard

 - on mobile web, we have duplicate hashtags

 - we should normalize for case insensitive hashtags

 - with relations, on mobile it is hard to confirm them

 - if I edit the text of a relation

 - when the notes are loading, I wish I could hit the plus button

 - when the notes are loading, I wish I could do search

 - can't click on tag to filter

 - the x inside the search is too small, need a X button

   - Fix the all notes button on the sidebar

 - should have @ with mention on left side and ~ with author on the right side

 - ability to search on multiple tags

 - cannot see the date on mobile

 - on mobile, everytime it loads the cursor position is reset

 - on mobile, the loading time is an issue since it is not a native app

 - "not sure when I should create a new note vs. split one" ~Ben Williams

 - "need to including trailing space when another word is there on autocomplete" ~Ben Williams

 - "need to not be able to create a blank ~" ~Ben Williams

 - "need to check how many notes are

Plus button & other text manipulation functionality as a bar above keyboard, similar to Google Docs (mobile) ~jm

Clickable hashtags ~jm

Mobile-desktop live sync / refresh button ~jm

#meeting Jacob <> Ben Williams 2020-12-01

Top Priorities for December Alpha Launch/Share to Investors. Also Touchstones #designPrinciple

  1. Make the app holistically snappy, slick, not buggy
  1. The Lowest friction knowledge capture 
  2. High quality text editor experience
  3. Ben, Danish, Jacob & everyone use the app
  1. Gee-wow touches

Target people:

  • Bill trenchard (investor, new user),
  • Danish (uses Web app on desktop some, thoughtstream native app on iOS),
  • Jacob  (uses Web app on desktop, thoughtstream native app on iOS) ,
  • David Bieber (uses thoughtstream iOS native, Roam on desktop)
  • Ali Tarraf (mgmt consultant)

Detail Breakout examples (more tweaks below)

Lowest friction knowledge capture

  • Mobile
  • Quick login and stay logged in on mobile
  • Bottom up autocomplete on mobile
  • Bar with # and @ etc
  • Instant load time

High quality text editor experience

  • iOS web app has to feel as good as native
  • No “position is undefined” errors or other
  • No need for login each time
  • Touch bar for # @ ~ ?
  • No typing lag
  • Cursor jumps to before tag when creating tags on mobile

Ben & everyone use the new app actively

  • Jacob, Danish blockers primarily around snappy/quality experience
  • Navigation / design tweaks eg clicking on hashtags to filter
  • Can’t start losing content when logged out with tiny warning of “JWT expired”

Gee-wow touches

  • Inline expansion
  • Import Jerry/ Mark Trexler’s Brains

        

Future/Defer

?never lose anything, history? – export covers this?

Offline mode

Changelog

Sort by Date

  • Ben to move to prod today

Feature Funnel 1

Legend: add #small #medium or #big if it’s a quickfix or not. “small?” means “I hypothesize this might be small. Please feel free to change.” Question mark after a feature request means “I’m not sure I even want this feature how it’s articulated, but need to think about it/welcome input”. Urgency top to bottom.

More Important

Less Important

 #small URLs get duplicated when you copy paste an entry with a URL

#small? Use hover intent or something similar to make it so "open link" button doesn't disappear while you are moving over to click a link https://github.com/briancherne/jquery-hoverIntent . Uses mouse acceleration

-on closing the page, a confirm dialog

No scrollbar on tags

#small? UX Polish On mobile Web:

-Tapping in the margins doesn’t move cursor, like a text file does

-Seemingly impossible to tap the cursor so it begins at start of doc

-Select all selects everything whereas most of the time I wanted to just select a note

#small? #tweak “collapse note” button

#medium? maybe show all the way to matching portion of text in <> autocomplete? I can’t see what I’m selecting. Would require multiline autocomplete results #tweak

User Interviews

Jacob Cole:

        Oh btw any more reflections from yesterday about why you seem to like the iOS app vs the web app?

I know you mentioned these, but I’m not sure these are the biggest things:

danish likes:

date stamp on iOS

tear away

dashed line

no unnecessary colors

likes "go back" instead of overlay

Wishlist: “Collapse” note option for long text

Some other things I found, and let me know if these are relevant to you

 

On mobile Web:

-Tapping in the margins doesn’t move cursor, like a text file does

-Seemingly impossible to tap the cursor so it begins at start of doc

-Select all selects everything whereas most of the time I wanted to just select a note

What else?

Danish Shabbir:

        File Transfer: IMG_4579.png

        This red bar is very nice

        The teal plus button is unpleasant

        File Transfer: IMG_4580.png

        File Transfer: IMG_4581.png

        Search and Add at bottom right are Great

        The back button to see tags is better than sidebar folding in on my notes

        Its just a nice motion

        Going elsewhere

        A level up in abstraction

        Whereas a sidebar coming to conceal my notes :((

        The experience and button placement on web vs mobile doesnt have to be identical

Jacob Cole:

        Operating on desktop, are you finding the thing usable? Or not so much?

Danish Shabbir:

        On my desktop I don’t feel much desire to use it.

        Idk why- I will try to consciously do it for a week

Jacob Cole:

        Wow amazing

        And please make it your medium to get creative with

        Would you like tips on how to use it the way I use it?

Danish Shabbir:

        

         I already had a nice moment

        I used. <> to find other things to be creative about

        And I remembered my interest in learning about tea supply chain

        Yes please tips

        Why don’t you write up a page of tricks and tips?

Jacob Cole:

        Yeah, I need to write an on boarding guide this week

        Including tips and tricks

Danish Shabbir:

        And just send me the tips

        now 😛

        And paste them into your on boarding doc

Jacob Cole:

        Well, you put down a word crepitus

Maybe add #Word

        I am imagine this may be obvious and you just didn’t do it

        Instead of saying <> book

        #book

Danish Shabbir:

        <> is for connecting notes yes?

Jacob Cole:

        Ya

Danish Shabbir:

        # and for tagging

Jacob Cole:

        Ya

Danish Shabbir:

        And @ for creating entities

        And ~ for attributing to entities?

Jacob Cole:

        I am weird but I  instead use @ to share things with people

        Like next time I talk to my mom why are all the things I am going to say to her?

        *what

        And then I can quickly zoom in to the context with @

        I don’t think it’s necessary to obsessively tag people. Just write their names

Danish Shabbir:

        why should I use tags?

        to make lists of things

        Things I’d remember tagged under their class

        Want to remember

Jacob Cole:

        #book #quote #restaurant

Danish Shabbir:

        

Jacob Cole:

        In terms of just writing my own reflections, I’m really looking forward to a future “untagged”

        Feature

        And/or a “ blank hashtag” which means: thing is salient, but I don’t know what category it should go in right now. What I typically do for that is #Thoughtstream Or #Reflection right now

Danish Shabbir:

        Cool !

Jacob Cole:

        Have you started any lists, like a list of quotes or ideas?

        Do you have any such lists externally? For instance A different application? We can paste them in

Danish Shabbir:

        When I switch to idea flow desktop

        And add a new note

        I’d like a fog over everything except the new note

        It breaks the flow to look at the previous thoughtstreeam

        Part of the pleasure of the phone app is the red thing brings attention to the place where note is being added

        And the options you have for adding that note

        It is more ligned with the flow

        

        My answer is 300% zoom

        This is my whole screen

Ideaflow Bug Tracker

[a]Ben: "So what's the desired behavior here? The user should not be given a file when they click the share button? We want to change the functionality to have all notes shown in the current view copied to the clipboard instead?"

Jacob: For me that's a fine call for now I think. It will make it usable on mobile, I believe. Maybe stick this under a feature flag for further consideration if it's at all an issue
